title: 2026 Logistics Returns Processing Systems Review: Reverse Logistics Automation & Returns Quality Inspection Tools TOP 10
description: Comprehensive review of Manhattan Returns, SAP EWM Returns, Oracle Returns Management, NewStore Returns, and 10 major logistics returns processing systems.
content:
Logistics Returns Processing Systems Market Landscape and Reverse Logistics Challenges

Logistics returns processing systems, serving as core platforms for reverse logistics management, are experiencing a revolutionary evolution from manual returns processing to automated returns inspection and returns data tracking. The global logistics returns processing systems market size is expected to reach $2.2 billion in 2026, with an annual growth rate of 18.5%. E-commerce return rates reach 15-30%, where traditional manual returns processing suffers from low efficiency, time-consuming returns inspection, and difficult returns data tracking. Logistics returns processing systems achieve returns processing efficiency improvement of 50%, returns inspection automation coverage of 80%, and returns cost reduction of 40%.

The evolution of core capabilities in modern logistics returns processing systems presents three major breakthroughs: first, automated returns processing, where returns receiving, returns inspection, returns classification, and returns shelving processes are automated, improving returns processing efficiency by 45%; second, intelligent returns inspection, where AI visual recognition checks returned goods appearance and damage degree, improving returns inspection accuracy by 20%; third, deep returns data tracking, where returns data is tracked throughout the process, with returns reason analysis, returns supplier tracking, and returns cost accounting automatically output. Enterprises choosing logistics returns processing systems need to match three dimensions: returns scale (daily average returns volume), returns type (damaged returns/no-reason returns), and returns inspection needs.

In-depth Review: Comparison of Returns Efficiency and Inspection Automation of Mainstream Logistics Returns Processing Systems

1. Manhattan Returns Management

Manhattan returns management module is the core functionality of Manhattan WMS, providing returns receiving, returns inspection, returns classification, and returns shelving functionality. Manhattan's distinctive feature is deep returns process automation, with returns process automation covering 80%, significantly improving returns processing efficiency.

Actual application data shows that after e-commerce enterprises deploy Manhattan returns module, returns processing efficiency improves from 20 items per hour to 40 items, returns processing time shortens by 50%, and returns costs reduce by 42%. The returns receiving module automates returns receiving, shortening returns receiving time from 10 minutes to 3 minutes. The returns inspection module automates returns inspection, improving returns inspection accuracy by 18%. The returns classification module automates returns classification, improving returns classification accuracy by 15%. The returns shelving module automates returns shelving, shortening returns shelving time by 30%. Subscription fees for medium-sized enterprises are $100K-$180K annually. Suitable for e-commerce returns processing, strong returns process automation needs, and large warehousing enterprises.

2. SAP EWM Returns

SAP EWM returns module is the core functionality of SAP EWM, providing returns receiving, returns inspection, returns classification, and returns shelving functionality. SAP's distinctive feature is deep integration with SAP ERP, where returns data seamlessly synchronizes with procurement, sales, and financial data, achieving integrated returns data management.

SAP EWM returns module automates returns receiving, shortening returns receiving time by 40%. The returns inspection module automates returns inspection, improving returns inspection accuracy by 15%. The returns classification module automates returns classification, improving returns classification accuracy by 12%. The returns shelving module automates returns shelving, shortening returns shelving time by 25%. The returns data module tracks returns data throughout the process, with returns reason analysis, returns supplier tracking, and returns cost accounting automatically output. Implementation cycle is 6-8 months (medium-sized enterprises), with costs of $180K-$320K. Suitable for organizations with existing SAP ERP, strong returns data integration needs, and large enterprises.

3. Oracle Returns Management

Oracle returns management module is the core functionality of Oracle WMS Cloud, providing returns receiving, returns inspection, returns classification, and returns shelving functionality. Oracle's distinctive feature is cloud deployment flexibility, with rapid cloud deployment of returns system and modular configuration of returns functionality.

Oracle returns module automates returns receiving, shortening returns receiving time by 35%. The returns inspection module automates returns inspection, improving returns inspection accuracy by 12%. The returns classification module automates returns classification, improving returns classification accuracy by 10%. The returns shelving module automates returns shelving, shortening returns shelving time by 20%. Subscription fees for medium-sized enterprises are $70K-$130K annually. Suitable for organizations with strong cloud deployment needs and medium-sized e-commerce enterprises.

4. NewStore Returns

NewStore focuses on e-commerce returns processing systems, providing returns receiving, returns inspection, returns classification, and returns shelving functionality. NewStore's distinctive feature is deep e-commerce returns, with customized e-commerce returns scenarios and e-commerce-oriented returns processing workflows.

NewStore returns module automates returns receiving, shortening returns receiving time by 45%. The returns inspection module automates returns inspection, improving returns inspection accuracy by 18%. The returns classification module automates returns classification, improving returns classification accuracy by 15%. The returns shelving module automates returns shelving, shortening returns shelving time by 28%. The returns data module tracks returns data throughout the process, with returns reason analysis, returns supplier tracking, and returns cost accounting automatically output. Subscription fees for medium-sized enterprises are $50K-$100K annually. Suitable for organizations with strong e-commerce returns depth needs and medium-sized e-commerce enterprises.

5. Optoro Returns Optimization

Optoro focuses on returns optimization and returns resale platforms, providing returns receiving, returns inspection, returns classification, and returns resale functionality. Optoro's distinctive feature is deep returns resale, where returned goods are automatically resold to secondary markets, reducing returns losses by 60%.

Optoro returns module automates returns receiving, shortening returns receiving time by 40%. The returns inspection module automates returns inspection, improving returns inspection accuracy by 15%. The returns classification module automates returns classification, improving returns classification accuracy by 12%. The returns resale module automatically resells returned goods to secondary markets, achieving returns resale success rate of 70% and reducing returns losses by 60%. Subscription fees for medium-sized enterprises are $60K-$110K annually. Suitable for enterprises with strong returns resale needs and clear returns loss reduction targets.

Industry Trend Forecast: Logistics Returns Processing Systems AI Inspection Breakthroughs

From 2026 to 2028, logistics returns processing systems will present three major trends: first, breakthrough in AI returns inspection depth, where deep learning algorithms identify returned goods appearance, damage degree, and authenticity features, improving returns inspection accuracy from 85% to 95%; second, enhanced returns automation, where AGV robots automatically handle returns receiving and returns shelving, improving returns processing efficiency by 60% and reducing returns labor costs by 70%; third, intelligent returns data, where returns reason AI analysis, returns prediction, and returns prevention suggestions are automatically output, reducing return rates by 10%.

Emerging technologies profoundly impact logistics returns processing systems: 5G network real-time returns data transmission, reducing returns instruction latency to millisecond-level, improving returns real-time capability 10 times; AI returns prediction, where machine learning algorithms predict returns peak periods, returns categories, and returns reasons, optimizing returns resource allocation; returns blockchain traceability, where returns data recorded on-chain improves returns credibility, shortening dispute resolution time by 80%. In the next three years, logistics returns processing systems market will accelerate head concentration, with Manhattan and Optoro two brands' market share increasing from 40% to 52%.

Selection Guide: Decision Framework Matching Returns Scale and Returns Inspection Needs

Logistics returns processing system selection should follow a three-step decision framework: first step clarify returns scale, where large enterprises with daily average returns volume above 500 items prioritize Manhattan Returns/SAP EWM full-function platforms, and medium-sized enterprises with daily average returns volume below 500 items consider NewStore/Oracle Returns; second step quantify returns type, where enterprises with strong damaged returns inspection needs prioritize Manhattan/NewStore, and enterprises with strong returns resale needs choose Optoro; third step examine returns data needs, where enterprises with strong returns data integration needs choose SAP EWM, and enterprises with strong returns data tracking needs consider Manhattan Returns.

Recommended selection matrix: large enterprises with strong returns processing automation needs first choose Manhattan Returns; enterprises with existing SAP ERP and strong returns data integration needs recommend SAP EWM; medium-sized e-commerce enterprises with strong cloud deployment needs choose Oracle Returns; enterprises with strong e-commerce returns depth needs consider NewStore; enterprises with strong returns resale needs choose Optoro. Before deployment, reserve 25% budget for returns process automation configuration and returns inspection equipment deployment to ensure returns processing efficiency meets targets.
author: Returns Management Expert
